examples.title: Simple Process Creation
examples.description: Demonstrates how a new process is created in the system model using fork system call
examples.code:
PROCEDURE CreateProcess(programName)
BEGIN
    pid ← fork()  // Create new process
    IF pid = 0 THEN
        // Child process
        loadProgram(programName)  // Load program into memory
        executeProgram()
    ELSE IF pid > 0 THEN
        // Parent process
        waitForChild(pid)  // Wait for child to complete
        PRINT "Child process completed"
    ELSE
        PRINT "Error: Process creation failed"
    END IF
END
examples.explanation: The fork system call creates a new process by duplicating the calling process. The child process gets a copy of the parent's address space, and both continue execution from the fork return point.

examples.title: Process States Simulation
examples.description: Python code demonstrating process state transitions in the system model
examples.code:
import time
from enum import Enum

class ProcessState(Enum):
    NEW = "NEW"
    READY = "READY"
    RUNNING = "RUNNING"
    WAITING = "WAITING"
    TERMINATED = "TERMINATED"

class Process:
    def __init__(self, pid, name):
        self.pid = pid
        self.name = name
        self.state = ProcessState.NEW
        self.program_counter = 0
        self.registers = {}
        self.memory = {}
    
    def load(self):
        self.state = ProcessState.READY
        print(f"Process {self.name} loaded into memory, state: {self.state.value}")
    
    def schedule(self):
        self.state = ProcessState.RUNNING
        print(f"Process {self.name} scheduled on CPU, state: {self.state.value}")
    
    def wait_for_io(self):
        self.state = ProcessState.WAITING
        print(f"Process {self.name} waiting for I/O, state: {self.state.value}")
    
    def io_complete(self):
        self.state = ProcessState.READY
        print(f"I/O complete, Process {self.name} ready, state: {self.state.value}")
    
    def terminate(self):
        self.state = ProcessState.TERMINATED
        print(f"Process {self.name} terminated, state: {self.state.value}")

# Demonstration
p = Process(101, "Editor")
p.load()
p.schedule()
p.wait_for_io()
p.io_complete()
p.schedule()
p.terminate()
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the five process states (NEW, READY, RUNNING, WAITING, TERMINATED) and how a process transitions between them during its lifecycle.

examples.title: System Call Example
examples.description: C program demonstrating system calls for process management and file I/O in the system model
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates several key system calls: fork() creates a new process, getpid() returns process ID, creat() creates a file, write() performs I/O, close() closes file descriptor, waitpid() makes parent wait for child. These show the interface between user mode and kernel mode.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for fork/creat, O(n) for write based on bytes
